title: Follow the symlink when mounting volumes
body:
<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read and understood our contributing guidelines
(https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) as well as ensuring
that all your commits are signed with `git commit -s`.
-->

#### What type of PR is this?

<!--
Please label this pull request according to what type of issue you are
addressing, especially if this is a release targeted pull request.

Uncomment only one `/kind <>` line, hit enter to put that in a new line, and
remove leading whitespace from that line:
-->

/kind bug

#### What this PR does / why we need it:

There are two issues that are fixed by this commit
- Use `os.Lstat` instead of `os.Stat` when checking the existence of the mountpoint. `os.Stat` will follow the symlink (in the host's root!) which is always wrong in our case. This also means if you happen to have the target directory in your host system, #2621 will not occur :stuck_out_tongue:
- If `srcPath` is a symlink, follow the symlink and update `srcPath` to its destination. This replicates Docker's behavior and fixes #2621.

#### How to verify it

Use the following Dockerfile

```
FROM alpine

# /targetdir may or may not exist. The existing code will handle it correctly.
RUN ln -s /targetdir /data
VOLUME /data

RUN cat /proc/mounts
```

#### Which issue(s) this PR fixes:

<!--
Automatically closes linked issue when PR is merged.
Uncomment the following comment block and include the issue
number or None on one line.
Usage: `Fixes #<issue number>`, or `Fixes (paste link of issue)`, or `None`.
-->

Fixes #2621

#### Special notes for your reviewer:

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes please follow the kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
None
```

comments.body:
Can you add a test?
LGTM
Thanks @zhaofengli

Ok, I think I missed something big here. There are existing tests under `tests/bud/symlink`, and they seem pretty rigorous. Many of them should have already caught this issue (tested manually), but they have been passing because we are not testing them with `--layers` to force it to initialize the volumes for each step (i.e. the same situation for Dockerfile B in #2621). For `--layers`, we are testing the feature separately in `use-layers` with no cases with volumes.

What would be the best option here? Duplicate the `symlink` tests to have variants with `--layers`?
@zhaofengli Yes duplicate the tests.
Yes please, add the `--layers` option in the tests.
I believe all vendor-related issues have been fixed and squashed the commits.

Hmm, interesting CI failure. My local branch is clean and it passes the tests when run with `./test_runner.sh bud.bats` (except for "bud with encrypted FROM image," which seems to require additional setup).
Can we get the CI to re-run? I can't seem to reproduce the failure there. It could be caused by my last force-push.

@zhaofengli I'll try to get the CI to re-run, but sometimes bors doesn't always honor our requests.  If worse comes to worse, make a small edit to a file, `git add`, and `git commit --amend -s`, remove the edit, then `git add`, `git commit --amend -s`, fetch, rebase and push will force a retest.  Admittedly ugly, but effective.

bors retry
I reran the test this morning, and it looks like all the symlink tests are failing.
@zhaofengli Are you still working on this?
Sorry, I've been very busy the last two weeks. Will take a look at the problem when I have time. It's very curious that everything seemed to work for me with rootful Buildah.
@zhaofengli can you please rebase and update the PR so we can get it moving along :)
Sure thing, I completely forgot about this PR. :disappointed: I no longer have the setup to test it so it will take a short while.

A friendly reminder that this PR had no activity for 30 days.
@zhaofengli Any progress?
